meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Claude Code Setup to Solve Project Block
content.seo_description: Overcome project paralysis with this intermediate Claude Code setup guide. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude CLI to ship SaaS apps faster than ever.
content.answer_block: To eliminate project paralysis, pivot from manual scaffolding to AI-assisted rapid prototyping. By integrating Antigravity for architecture generation and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you create a feedback loop that transforms abstract concepts into deployable codebases in hours, not weeks. This workflow is the ultimate freelancer force multiplier.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat monitor for a dead project. The client is waiting, the deadline is encroaching, and your creative well has run dry. The 'no project ideas' syndrome isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of a high-velocity execution framework.

True mastery isn't about having the perfect idea; it's about having the perfect system to manifest any idea into a high-value MVP. By mastering Claude Code and Antigravity, you stop being a programmer who struggles to start and become an AI Architect who builds on demand. Let’s turn that blank screen into a production-ready repository.

steps.title: Architecting the Concept
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define the technical requirements and project structure before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a high-performance SaaS architecture for a [Project Type] that solves [User Pain Point]. Include folder structure, tech stack, and key API endpoints.

steps.title: Scaffolding the Foundation
steps.description: Automate the boilerplate setup using Claude Code to ensure industry-standard security and scalability patterns are baked in from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure authentication middleware for Next.js 15 using Supabase. Focus on RBAC and type-safe user sessions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Implement auth middleware based on the Antigravity spec provided in the root directory."

steps.title: Iterative Feature Injection
steps.description: Break the project into granular tasks and let Claude Code implement them sequentially to maintain high code quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the core dashboard feature into 5 distinct, testable user stories with corresponding database schema changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude task "Build the dashboard UI using the generated schema and Tailwind CSS components."

steps.title: Refining with AI Context
steps.description: Inject your project's specific context into Claude Code to ensure the generated features match your client's unique branding and requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase and suggest 3 optimizations for performance and 2 for developer experience improvement.

steps.title: Final Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final audit, generate documentation, and prepare the repository for production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive README.md and a deployment guide for Vercel, ensuring all environment variables are documented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Create a documentation suite and audit the project for deployment-ready best practices."

pro_tips: Always keep a 'brain.md' file in your root directory; point Claude Code to it so it maintains context across long sessions.
Use Antigravity to generate pseudo-code first, then feed that into Claude Code to prevent the LLM from hallucinating implementation details.
Alias your Claude Code CLI commands to your shell config to save 20-30 seconds on every interaction cycle.
When Claude Code gets stuck, use the 'claude explain' command to force it to re-evaluate the current logic tree.
Never accept the first output; always ask for 'three alternative approaches' to ensure you are choosing the most performant architectural path.
content.next_steps: Once the build is complete, deploy to a staging environment and run a smoke test. Then, replicate this workflow for your next client project to refine your speed.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'blank page' kill your freelance career. Build faster, charge higher premiums, and automate the mundane so you can focus on the architecture that wins clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
